137185.explanation: We see problems with traffic safety, but a general ban goes too far. We want enforcement of speed and behavior, clear rules for e-bikes and fatbikes. The problem is dangerous behavior, not the type of bike.

137186.explanation: We believe cameras can support at specific locations, but they never replace people. We strictly protect privacy.

137187.explanation: Yes, this is necessary due to the housing shortage, long waiting lists, and affordability. The market will not solve this on its own. Without strict requirements for social housing, starters, our youth, and low-income groups remain sidelined.

137188.explanation: There is a large lack of housing, and offices remain empty for long periods. This vacancy can easily be solved by converting them into housing.

137189.explanation: Everyone has the right to a good, healthy, and affordable home and should not be pushed out of our neighborhoods by the housing shortage. Only in urgent cases do we allow temporary living in holiday parks, always being mindful of exploitation and abuse of power.

137192.explanation: We believe that construction should be based on need and where necessary. This means there must be enough homes, including for starters.

137193.explanation: The plans are not yet clear, the costs and feasibility are uncertain, and it has not yet been developed. The building has architectural value. We take that into account, but if there are more reasons to demolish, we do not rule out choosing demolition.

137194.explanation: We are in favor of (targeted) conversion of farmland into nature, but not through forced expropriation without prospects for farmers.

137195.explanation: Poverty is not a natural phenomenon. It is not a storm that hits us or an earthquake that cannot be prevented. Poverty arises from human choices, how we structure our economy, how we distribute wealth, and who we do or do not protect.

137196.explanation: We oppose a mandatory counterperformance where people on social assistance benefits are required to perform community work. People on social assistance have the right to support, not punishment! Mandatory work in exchange for benefits amounts to forced labor and often leads to displacement.

137198.explanation: Education increases the chance of sustainable employment. Applying and learning at the same time is often counterproductive; municipalities should trust and give space.

137200.explanation: Affordable mobility for people with a low income. Mobility should not become a new form of inequality. We commit ourselves to affordable public transport, fair parking costs, and tailor-made solutions for those dependent on transport, such as people with disabilities, the elderly, and caregivers.

137201.explanation: Traffic measures are not imposed from above. Residents are involved from the start in the redesign of streets, play areas, parking policies, and traffic plans. Local knowledge is leading, not a spreadsheet.

137202.explanation: Parking must remain affordable. Car owners should not be seen as cash cows. We put maximum pressure on commercial parking companies to lower their rates and ensure the municipality itself provides sufficient affordable parking spaces.

137203.explanation: The city center will not be declared low-car as long as there is no good, affordable, and accessible alternative in the form of public transport and safe cycling routes. Low-car policies should never lead to exclusion. Cars remain accessible where necessary, especially for residents, people with disabilities, and suppliers.

137204.explanation: In neighborhoods where parking pressure is already high, the SP finds it unjust if ordinary parking spaces are converted into exclusive charging spots. For many residents with petrol or diesel cars, this feels like an additional restriction. Charging spots must be coordinated with the neighborhood, not imposed.

137205.explanation: Outside the center of Apeldoorn, we will not implement paid parking.

137206.explanation: First, public transport. We believe that a city center can only become car-free or low-car if public transport is free or very cheap and the frequency is high enough. Healthcare workers, caregivers, and people with disabilities must always be able to drive and park in the center.

137207.explanation: We favor 30 km/h zones in residential areas and around schools. We do not believe that everything within the ring road must be 30 km/h. SP Apeldoorn believes that, for example, traffic flow should also be considered.

137208.explanation: We believe that first the future need should be reviewed. If it turns out that two by two lanes are not necessary, we do not want to spend money on this. There is also the chance that the possible traffic jam problem will be shifted to another part of the Laan van Spitsbergen.

137209.explanation: Apeldoorn is and remains a cycling city. We invest in safe, well-maintained, and logical cycling routes between neighborhoods, villages, and the center. With extra attention for school routes, routes for the elderly, and busy crossings. This leads to a safe cycling action plan.

137210.explanation: Apeldoorn is a healthy city, so cyclists and pedestrians should get green lights more often. However, we prefer the use of smart traffic lights because cars that wait a long time at a traffic light continue to emit unnecessarily, which is bad for the environment.

137211.explanation: The SP is critical of wood burning, wants stricter rules and phase-out in residential areas, and believes the government should provide affordable, clean alternatives so people do not have to burn wood. As long as these are not available, we are also not in favor of a ban.

137212.explanation: Caregivers deserve more appreciation than a symbolic amount. €50 per year does not do justice to the time, effort, and costs of caregiving. An increase to €150 is modest but fair. Caregivers save the municipality and the healthcare system a lot of money.

137213.explanation: Caregiving is heavy and indispensable work. People who provide intensive care should not be put under extra pressure. Mandatory job applications in such situations are inhumane and unrealistic. The SP wants tailor-made solutions and exemption from the job application obligation as long as caregiving is needed.

137214.explanation: We believe no one should have to sleep on the streets. Municipalities have a duty of care to arrange enough shelter places for homeless people. If there are not enough beds, it is the municipality's responsibility to provide more.

137215.explanation: We are against hunting for recreation, but believe intervention is allowed if truly necessary for safety or damage reduction.

137216.explanation: The housing shortage cannot be solved otherwise. Inner-city locations are demonstrably insufficient.

137217.explanation: Although we believe that large companies and wealthy property owners are responsible for their buildings, the party supports financial aid to small entrepreneurs (SMEs) for this purpose.

233683.explanation: We are not fundamentally opposed to a property tax increase to maintain facilities. The condition is that the burden is fairly distributed and low incomes are protected. First, cuts must be made in prestige and marketing.

233684.explanation: We believe the national government should fully pay for tasks. Municipalities should not have to contribute their own funds. At the same time, residents should not suffer from shortages as long as The Hague underfunds.

233686.explanation: We strongly support municipal co-financing of heating networks but impose one hard condition: the heating network must be entirely or largely publicly owned. Energy is a basic need and should not be left to the free market.

233687.explanation: SP Apeldoorn does not favor a strict closing time for terraces. Terraces also have a social aspect and enhance social cohesion. We believe that this can go hand in hand with living in the city.

233688.explanation: We are strong advocates of a strong public government that is available to its residents in times of need. Safety and shelter should not be a luxury, but a basic right for every resident.

233689.explanation: Given the housing crisis, we as SP Apeldoorn want to maintain the ambition to have enough affordable, healthy, and starter homes. This cannot be achieved within the highways, so building over the highway is also necessary.

233690.explanation: We believe the municipality should actively support initiatives for housing forms for the elderly. This helps seniors live independently longer and combats loneliness. Think of care neighborhood centers like those in Oss.

233691.explanation: We oppose stopping as a rainbow municipality. Everyone must be able to be themselves and feel safe. Equal rights and acceptance are not symbolic but a municipal responsibility.

233694.explanation: We support extra investments in aid services for people with confused behavior. Good care, shelter, and guidance prevent escalation, nuisance, and ensure safety for themselves and their surroundings. National policy of 'everyone into the neighborhoods' fails and benefits no one.

137153.explanation: This is desirable from a democratic perspective. A referendum is an important tool for citizen participation. It enables residents to directly influence political decision-making. When the threshold is too high, this right becomes practically inaccessible.

137156.explanation: Associations know best what is needed for good maintenance and efficient use. This can save costs and ensures better tailored facilities. Additionally, it increases the responsibility and involvement of associations with their sports accommodation.

137158.explanation: The municipality should abolish the dog tax. This tax is unfair because dog owners pay for facilities often used by all residents. Furthermore, the tax discourages pet ownership, while dogs contribute to wellbeing and exercise.

137159.explanation: New business parks create more jobs and a strong local economy. At the same time, the municipality can plan for sufficient and appropriate housing, so living and working remain well balanced.

137160.explanation: Entrepreneurs know best when there is customer demand and can respond flexibly. This strengthens entrepreneurship and the local economy without the municipality imposing unnecessary rules. Strict enforcement is necessary in case of nuisance.

137161.explanation: Free opening hours ensure fair competition because all entrepreneurs get the same opportunity to adapt to demand. With clear, uniform local rules, policy remains transparent and enforceable without unnecessary restrictions for businesses.

137162.explanation: The municipality must prevent all agricultural land in Apeldoorn from being converted into nature reserves. There must remain space to use this land, where appropriate and carefully considered, to address the housing shortage for the growing population.

137164.explanation: Local energy projects accelerate the energy transition and reduce energy costs for residents. Additionally, they increase involvement and support for sustainable solutions, as residents take joint responsibility for their energy supply.

137165.explanation: Distribution prevents concentration in a few neighborhoods and promotes integration and social cohesion. This gives status holders more opportunities to participate in society and keeps livability balanced in all districts and villages. Attention is needed for a fair distribution among all housing seekers.

137166.explanation: This maintains greenery in the municipality and compensates for nature loss. Extra trees improve air quality, increase biodiversity, and create a pleasant living environment for residents.

137167.explanation: This encourages involvement and responsibility among residents for their living environment. The principle should be that local maintenance is supported by the neighborhood and costs do not increase.

137168.explanation: Agricultural land is scarce and urgently needed for food production and housing. Placing solar panels here wastes valuable space. Sustainable energy is better generated on rooftops of homes, businesses, and business parks.

137169.explanation: An environmental zone can lead to extra costs for residents and entrepreneurs who must replace their vehicles. This limits accessibility to the city center and can harm local shops, while cleaner alternatives can be encouraged in other ways.

137171.explanation: Although tourism generates income for entrepreneurs, the additional revenues do not always outweigh the costs, such as enforcement and pressure on nature and infrastructure. By stopping, Apeldoorn can choose quality and manageable tourism where residents' livability is central.

137172.explanation: But cameras for safety should be allowed. Camera surveillance can help prevent and solve crime. This is acceptable as long as the purpose is clear, privacy is protected, and camera use is strictly limited to safety.

137173.explanation: The Zwitsalter site along the Apeldoorn Canal can remain an event venue, provided the site is suitable for large-scale events without direct nuisance to neighbors.

137174.explanation: Do not automatically spend more money on integration of newcomers, but first investigate whether the current budget is used effectively. It is important to assess whether existing integration programs truly contribute to language skills, work, and societal participation.

137176.explanation: Sports are important for the health, development, and social contacts of children. Providing financial support gives all children equal opportunities to participate, regardless of their parents' income.

137177.explanation: Playing music contributes to the health, development, and social contacts of children. Providing financial support gives all children equal opportunities to participate, regardless of their parents' income.

137178.explanation: These events cost a lot of money and often only reach a limited group of residents. Municipal funds can be better spent on grassroots sports and facilities from which more residents directly benefit.

137179.explanation: Extra community enforcement officers (BOAs) provide more supervision in neighborhoods and the center, which can reduce crime and nuisance. This increases residents' sense of safety and contributes to a livable municipality.

137180.explanation: The municipality must allow new coffeeshops in Apeldoorn, but as limited as possible. Strict conditions and a limited number of coffeeshops ensure control, safety, and less nuisance for neighbors.

137181.explanation: This increases their personal safety in threatening situations and makes them more resilient without immediately using heavy means. With proper training and strict rules, pepper spray can be used responsibly and proportionally.

137182.explanation: Good lighting increases safety and the feeling of security, especially in the evening hours. Additionally, it makes public spaces more accessible and pleasant for residents to use in the evenings.

137183.explanation: Wolves cause insecurity and create much unrest among residents. Intervening is only necessary to protect humans and maintain support for nature policy.

137184.explanation: The municipality must ban carbide shooting during New Year's Eve, unless done by trained individuals at a permitted location. This limits safety risks and nuisance while allowing the tradition to continue under strict conditions.

137185.explanation: Fatbikes cause unsafe situations in crowded pedestrian areas due to their speed and size. A ban increases safety and prevents nuisance in the city center.

137186.explanation: Camera surveillance helps prevent illegal dumping and tackle offenders. This results in a cleaner living environment and lowers cleanup costs, while making the waste collection system fairer.

137187.explanation: There is a large shortage of affordable housing, especially for people with low or middle incomes. Because the national average is 28%, a higher share of social rental housing helps keep living affordable and shortens waiting lists. This allows Apeldoorn to better respond to local housing needs.

137188.explanation: The municipality should invest in converting vacant offices into homes. This can address the housing shortage faster without using additional space. Furthermore, reuse prevents vacancy and creates lively neighborhoods.

137189.explanation: Use of holiday homes as permanent residences should be allowed in a limited way, provided a fair financial contribution is paid to the municipality. This creates extra housing while preventing speculation and maintaining financial fairness in the housing market.

137192.explanation: This keeps the scale of residential neighborhoods fitting the green and village-like character of the city. At the same time, building along the railway enables efficient densification in well-accessible locations, with less impact on livability and the environment.

137193.explanation: The financial risks are too great. The building is outdated and difficult to adapt to modern requirements for living and working. Demolition offers space for new construction that better meets Apeldoorn's current housing needs and sustainability goals.

137194.explanation: The housing market shortage makes this necessary. Exceptions apply if infill is possible and construction is not allowed in certain areas because these lands have great landscape value.

137195.explanation: Participation is important for wellbeing. We must prevent further polarization in society.

137196.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits should be encouraged to be socially active in return. This promotes participation and increases the chances of gaining work experience and social contacts. Personal circumstances must always be taken into account.

137198.explanation: People with social assistance benefits who are taking further training do not always have to apply for jobs. Training increases the chances of sustainable employment and prevents people from remaining stuck on social assistance. By temporarily allowing time for education, the municipality invests in future prospects.

137200.explanation: Accessible public transport makes it possible to work, go to school, and participate in society. This increases opportunities and prevents social exclusion.

137201.explanation: Agree, provided there is sufficient support among local residents. This can contribute to a healthier and safer living environment for both children and residents. More greenery and play areas enhance livability and encourage neighborhood interaction.

137202.explanation: Makes it attractive and feasible to come to the city center by car. Especially for residents from neighborhoods or villages with less good public transport connections, this improves accessibility. Additionally, it lowers the threshold for short visits to shops and facilities.

137203.explanation: Not everyone can or wants to travel by public transport or bicycle, such as the elderly, families, and people with disabilities. Good car accessibility ensures that shops, hospitality, and facilities remain accessible to all residents.

137204.explanation: The municipality must designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars. This makes electric driving more accessible and supports the transition to cleaner transport. Sufficient charging points prevent search traffic and ensure fair use of public space.

137205.explanation: It can lead to parking pressure in adjacent neighborhoods where paid parking does not yet apply. Additionally, it can reduce accessibility for informal caregivers, family, and small entrepreneurs. Without good alternatives and customization, paid parking can undermine support among residents.

137206.explanation: Car-free zones create more space for pedestrians, cyclists, and terraces, which increases safety and livability. Additionally, air quality improves and the city center becomes more attractive to visitors and residents.

137207.explanation: The maximum speed on more roads within the inner ring should in principle be 30 km/h. This increases traffic safety and makes the city center more livable. Exceptions should apply to roads with a clear traffic flow function to maintain accessibility.

137208.explanation: More lanes lead to additional traffic (induced demand), increasing nuisance in surrounding neighborhoods. Moreover, widening requires much space, which can come at the expense of greenery (tree removal) and cyclist and pedestrian safety. Extra car traffic also does not fit with a sustainable mobility policy.

137209.explanation: Apeldoorn must build more separated bike paths. By separating cyclists and motor traffic, traffic safety greatly increases, especially for children and the elderly. Additionally, this makes cycling more comfortable and attractive, contributing to less car traffic and a livable city.

137210.explanation: Cyclists and pedestrians should more often get green lights at traffic signals. This improves safety and flow for vulnerable road users. It also encourages walking and cycling as sustainable and healthy transport choices.

137211.explanation: The municipality must limit burning wood in stoves and fireplaces in Apeldoorn depending on weather conditions. Wood burning causes air pollution and health complaints, especially for people with lung problems. A ban contributes to cleaner air and a healthier living environment.

137212.explanation: Informal caregivers make a great and often intensive contribution to caring for others. A higher allowance is recognition of their effort and helps prevent overload.

137213.explanation: People on social assistance who are informal caregivers should not always have to keep applying for jobs. Informal care takes much time and energy and is of great social importance. By applying customization and temporarily granting exemptions, the municipality prevents overload and recognizes the value.

137214.explanation: The municipality must provide more shelters for homeless and roofless people. Adequate shelter is needed to offer people safety, rest, and support. This helps stabilize their situation and increases the chance of recovery and transition to suitable housing.

137215.explanation: The municipality should stop hunting wild animals on municipal land.

137216.explanation: New construction outside the built-up area can possibly be an option. Due to the great housing shortage, extra space is needed to build sufficient homes. With careful integration, new construction can contribute to affordable housing without unnecessarily damaging the landscape.

137217.explanation: The municipality should help entrepreneurs make their buildings more accessible to people with disabilities if necessary. Accessible buildings ensure everyone can independently participate in social and economic life. Financial support lowers the threshold for the entrepreneur.

233683.explanation: For businesses, this tax burden is much higher than in surrounding municipalities, so further increases are not good for the competitive position of entrepreneurs. For private individuals, this applies to a lesser extent.

233684.explanation: Co-management tasks should be financed by the government on a cost-recovery basis. see GW 124 in conjunction with article 108 of the Municipalities Act.

233686.explanation: There are concerns about the size of the investment, the intended return, and the support among residents in the relevant neighborhoods.

233687.explanation: If there is demonstrably no nuisance, that is an option because a lot of noise nuisance is experienced from the night hospitality sector. For citizens who have complaints, filing a report is a too complicated and discouraging process. Enforcement on this is necessary.

233688.explanation: If that is a task of the municipality and assuming that these provisions are missing.

233689.explanation: When infill possibilities have been fully utilized and the housing shortage has not been solved thereby, building on the other side of the A1 or A50 is an option.

233690.explanation: Living at home longer is the policy and for that elderly housing is needed. Housing forms such as a ‘knarrenhof’ should be facilitated as soon as possible.

233691.explanation: This contributes to safety, acceptance, and equal opportunities for a group that still faces discrimination and exclusion. By providing support, the municipality promotes social cohesion and ensures everyone can be themselves and participate in society.

233694.explanation: Unfortunately, this is increasing. Prevention is better than cure. People have the right to appropriate care, not neglect. Public safety may also be at risk. Without structural improvements in organization and cooperation, it will not succeed.

137153.explanation: NSC wants to significantly lower the threshold for referenda by at least halving the required number of signatures. This gives every citizen a fair chance to exert influence and keeps participation from being a privilege for a few.

137156.explanation: NSC is cautious about transferring sports facilities to associations. This can only happen if there is sufficient capacity and guaranteed social value. The municipality remains responsible for accessibility and preservation of facilities in neighborhoods and villages.

137158.explanation: NSC is in favor of abolishing the dog tax. They consider the basis outdated and believe it is insufficiently justifiable that only dog owners pay tax, while this does not apply to other pets.

137159.explanation: NSC recognizes the need for more business parks and office space in Apeldoorn and the Stedendriehoek region, given the expected population growth and required employment. They support expansion, provided it does not harm livability.

137160.explanation: NSC wants to maintain the current opening hours (Sun-Thu 06:00-01:00, Fri-Sat until 02:00). Permits until 04:00 remain possible. This provides lively hospitality and protects night rest. NSC chooses balance, not full freedom.

137161.explanation: NSC wants to keep the number of shopping Sundays the same as the current situation. Local shopkeepers should have maximum freedom to decide whether to open on Sundays. They are therefore not in favor of fewer shopping Sundays but also not for expansion.

137162.explanation: NSC supports facilitating the transformation of farmland into nature reserves. They are not against conversion but see this as an opportunity to strengthen nature and biodiversity.

137164.explanation: NSC supports financially helping residents who want to jointly generate sustainable energy. Municipal subsidies are available for citizen initiatives to generate sustainable energy, such as solar panels.

137165.explanation: NSC aims for an equal distribution of status holders across villages and neighborhoods to enable maximum integration. Where needed, NSC makes extra funds available for the integration of newcomers.

137166.explanation: NSC Apeldoorn strongly focuses on the preservation and strengthening of greenery in the municipality. When trees are cut down, NSC considers it important that the loss is compensated by planting extra trees. This keeps Apeldoorn a green, healthy, and attractive living environment for everyone.

137167.explanation: NSC supports subsidizing residents who want to maintain green spaces in their neighborhood themselves or have a plan to green their neighborhood. They should be eligible for municipal subsidies.

137168.explanation: NSC wants to be cautious with solar parks on farmland. First, we stimulate solar panels on roofs of private individuals, businesses, and municipal buildings. We do not prohibit it, but the preference clearly lies with other locations.

137169.explanation: NSC supports introducing a zero-emission zone for delivery vans and trucks in the city center from 2030, to contribute to national climate goals. NSC is therefore not in favor of withdrawing this plan.

137171.explanation: NSC believes the municipality can continue to invest in campaigns to attract tourists and day-trippers to Apeldoorn, as long as the benefits for the local community outweigh the disadvantages.

137172.explanation: NSC is critical of collecting additional data in public spaces. They believe data should only be collected when necessary and that residents' privacy must always be guaranteed. NSC advocates prudence and transparency.

137173.explanation: NSC supports the development of the Zwitsal site into an area that allows both housing and space for recreation and events ('Zwitsal Buitenstad'). The site may therefore also remain an event venue.

137174.explanation: NSC Apeldoorn allocates extra funds where needed for the integration of newcomers. They aim for maximum integration through spreading newcomers across neighborhoods and villages and invest in support where necessary.

137176.explanation: NSC is in favor of subsidies for sports club memberships for children of parents/caregivers with low incomes (minimum income households).

137177.explanation: NSC wants every child to be able to take music lessons, regardless of their parents' income. Municipal subsidies make this possible for low-income families. Music connects, develops talent, and increases opportunities. We invest in that.

137178.explanation: NSC believes the municipality can continue investing in top sports events, as long as the crowding and logistics do not cause disproportionate nuisance to the surroundings.

137179.explanation: NSC supports extra deployment of neighborhood police officers and municipal enforcement officers (boa’s) who oversee safety and neighborhood prevention.

137180.explanation: NSC is against allowing new coffeeshops. They want to maintain the existing supply and allow no expansion.

137181.explanation: NSC supports providing enforcement officers (boa’s) who monitor safety with appropriate means, if desired and possibly also equipped with pepper spray. It is important to listen carefully to the experiences and needs of the enforcement officers themselves.

137182.explanation: NSC is in favor of better lighting of squares, parks, and other places where citizens may feel unsafe in the evening and at night.

137183.explanation: NSC acknowledges that wolves sometimes pose a threat to humans and animals. In such cases, dispersal and active population management must be possible. Shooting is seen only as an ultimate last resort, and solely based on a provincial assessment. The municipality can bring this to the province’s attention if necessary.

137184.explanation: NSC wants to preserve local traditions, such as carb shooting during the New Year’s celebrations. They are therefore against a ban on carb shooting.

137185.explanation: NSC wants to prevent nuisance in walking areas. During shopping hours, a fatbike ban should be possible, as in Enschede. A total ban is too drastic and difficult to enforce. NSC opts for limiting negative effects and strict enforcement.

137186.explanation: NSC supports installing cameras at waste collection points to prevent nuisance caused by littering.

137187.explanation: NSC wants the municipality to invest in housing projects with at least two-thirds affordable homes for middle incomes, of which 30% are social rental housing. This ensures a balanced housing market and prevents exclusion.

137188.explanation: NSC believes long-term vacancy should be prevented, especially considering the enormous shortage of housing.

137189.explanation: Permanent living in holiday homes only provides temporary relief and causes pressure on facilities and fragmented policies. NSC chooses sustainable solutions: speeding up new construction and repurposing vacant buildings for affordable, quality homes.

137192.explanation: Apeldoorn offers various locations where high-rise buildings can be responsibly incorporated. It is essential that these developments always take place in close consultation with the involved neighborhood and residents to create support and ensure livability.

137193.explanation: This building is valuable heritage, closely connected to Apeldoorn's history. Its current condition requires immediate action. NSC wants to realize a suitable destination in the short term to guarantee preservation and future-proof use.

137194.explanation: NSC believes that repurposing agricultural land is often well suited for housing, given the enormous housing shortage.

137195.explanation: NSC wants support for low incomes to be efficient and fair. Lower expenditures are possible through targeted help that promotes self-reliance. We combat waste and bureaucracy but continue to advocate a solid safety net for those who really need it.

137196.explanation: If a citizen is unable to provide their own income, they have the right to social assistance benefits under certain conditions. A work-exchange or reciprocal performance does not fit this right.

137198.explanation: NSC believes this can vary per situation. In many cases, it is appropriate to continue job searching.

137200.explanation: Public transport is a provincial utility service. A good way to support it is by using the city pass for low-income residents.

137201.explanation: No generic position can be formulated here. This must be assessed per situation/neighborhood.

137202.explanation: Parking fees in Apeldoorn are reasonable (also compared to other places).

137203.explanation: Car accessibility to the city center is important for economic vitality and accessibility, especially for entrepreneurs, large purchases, and people with limited mobility.

137204.explanation: No generic position can be formulated here. This must be assessed per situation/neighborhood.

137205.explanation: Paid parking increases costs for residents and visitors and can shift parking pressure to surrounding streets.

137206.explanation: NSC believes that accessibility of the city center is more important. Moreover, there are enough car-free streets.

137207.explanation: Where traffic safety makes it necessary, sufficient measures have already been taken, and 30 km/h zones have been introduced. Further expansion is not desirable at this time.

137208.explanation: NSC agrees with the research and advice. Traffic flow (with 2 x 1 lanes) can be achieved well at lower costs.

137209.explanation: Apeldoorn has excellent cycling paths. Where traffic safety falls short, NSC is willing to invest in improvements. This way, we ensure safe and accessible routes for everyone.

137210.explanation: Traffic flow must be considered per situation. We do not necessarily see priority for more green lights for cyclists.

137211.explanation: NSC wants to focus on practical solutions and citizen responsibility. No total ban, but awareness and personal responsibility of residents. Possibly support for education about the disadvantages of wood smoke, but not for strict enforcement or fines.

137212.explanation: NSC supports a significant increase in the current allowance for informal caregivers but does not think this increase should automatically be set at €150. It may also be higher.

137213.explanation: Informal care is often an intensive and indispensable task that requires a lot of time and energy. A mandatory job application requirement puts extra pressure on these people and can seriously reduce the quality of care for vulnerable individuals.

137214.explanation: NSC believes we must prioritize this issue, with sufficient care places as a precondition.

137215.explanation: NSC values careful management of nature and heritage, where humans and animals coexist in balance. Alternative measures for population management must always be explored in consultation with experts and residents.

137216.explanation: NSC wants to prioritize new homes and neighborhoods.

137217.explanation: NSC strives for minimal requirements for the accessibility of buildings with a social function and shops.

233683.explanation: NSC commits to limiting increases in property tax (ozb) as much as possible and — where financial capacity allows — even to reducing it. This keeps housing costs affordable for Apeldoorn residents and prevents unnecessary pressure on families.

233684.explanation: NSC wants a complete and clear overview of all national government tasks the municipality must execute, including associated funding. Our principle is simple: tasks assigned by the national government are paid for by the national government. The municipality does not use its own funds to finance national government tasks.

233686.explanation: The municipality will only contribute to the construction and operation of heat networks in neighborhoods where this can be demonstrably effective and affordable. This is not always possible at acceptable costs within existing infrastructure.

233687.explanation: NSC Apeldoorn opposes extending terrace opening hours. Current rules already offer entrepreneurs plenty of freedom and protect the peace of local residents. Only in exceptional cases can a one-time extension be requested via a permit.

233688.explanation: Ensuring a safe and clean living environment is a core government task. Therefore, the municipality must provide enough shelter locations and other necessary facilities to protect residents from the effects of disasters.

233689.explanation: Providing sufficient housing is a government responsibility and thus also a municipal task. Existing green spaces must remain intact, and new green areas should be created where possible.

233690.explanation: Providing enough living space — including for the elderly — is a government task. Therefore, the municipality must cooperate with initiatives and actively develop new housing solutions.

233691.explanation: Subsidies must be used efficiently and always be assessed equally without discrimination on grounds of religion, belief, political preference, race, gender, disability, sexual orientation, or other criteria (Article 1 of the Constitution).

233694.explanation: Assistance for people with confused behavior is part of healthcare and requires specific knowledge and skills. It is important to prevent this care from being seen as a police task. Professional caregivers must take the lead.

137153.explanation: Of course, the threshold should not be so low that any small group can easily force a referendum. You have a right to administrative peace and clarity.

137156.explanation: We oppose transferring the management of sports facilities to clubs. That leads to fragmentation, higher costs, and extra pressure on volunteers. Management requires specialized knowledge. Clubs should be supported and appreciated, not burdened with extra tasks.

137158.explanation: We are against such indirect taxes that do not correspond to direct municipal services. Therefore, the dog tax should be abolished unless every euro is demonstrably spent on good dog-walking areas, fields, and appropriate facilities that residents truly benefit from.

137159.explanation: We want more space for business parks through densification and better utilization, so good agricultural land is not sacrificed. New plots are for Apeldoorn entrepreneurs and local SMEs, not for low-employment tech companies that occupy a lot of space but create few jobs.

137160.explanation: Entrepreneurs should determine their own opening hours. We will, however, consider social safety per area and prevent groups from relocating to the last open bar. Freedom to operate goes hand in hand with responsibility and good coordination with the municipality and neighborhood.

137161.explanation: We believe entrepreneurs should decide for themselves whether to open on Sundays, as long as this fits with the livability of the area. Entrepreneurs also have a responsibility toward the residents of their village, neighborhood, or district.

137162.explanation: We want to prevent valuable agricultural land from being converted into 'desired nature.' Apeldoorn already has enough nature with the Veluwe and surrounding rural areas.

137164.explanation: We support local energy initiatives by making permits and advice cheaper, not through municipal subsidies for installations.

137165.explanation: We oppose a mandatory distribution policy because we see status holders as ordinary residents: no priority on the housing market, no guided placement, but consideration of where someone can best establish themselves within society.

137166.explanation: We support nature but oppose a fixed rule that two new trees must be planted for every cut tree. This is unrealistic, requires more space, and makes housing more expensive. This could turn Apeldoorn into one large forest over time instead of a livable, affordable municipality.

137167.explanation: Agreed if you and your neighborhood truly maintain the greenery yourselves over the long term and can be held accountable so that it does not result in extra costs or workload for the municipality.

137168.explanation: We want to ban solar panels on good agricultural land. All roofs must be fully used first, preference given to multiple uses of space, and only in exceptional cases according to the national 'no, unless' ladder, where the primary function truly remains agricultural and not three sheep under a solar panel.

137169.explanation: An environmental zone in the city center mostly results in extra rules and detours. This affects residents, entrepreneurs, and suppliers. We want no symbolic politics or unnecessary bans, but an accessible and livable city center where people and businesses can do their work.

137171.explanation: We do not want to stop tourism campaigns. Tourism is important for the local economy and entrepreneurs in recreation, hospitality, and retail. Targeted promotion creates employment, vital holiday parks, and space for new initiatives. Less promotion harms Apeldoorn.

137172.explanation: We believe the municipality should stop collecting additional data in public spaces if it does not demonstrably add value. Privacy and trust come first. Data collection is only acceptable where it truly contributes to safety and is well justified.

137173.explanation: The Zwitsalterrein must remain an event site. Apeldoorn needs space for large events such as the Dragon Boat Festival. The site is central and easily accessible. This requires tailored solutions, consultation with residents, and limiting nuisance. Clarity in advance is fair to everyone.

137174.explanation: We do not want to automatically spend money on integration. First, it must be clear who is temporary and who becomes a permanent part of our society. Only then can support be efficient. Spending money without demonstrable results is pointless. We choose customized and effective support.

137176.explanation: All children must be able to play sports, even if there is little money at home. Therefore, we support existing, effective programs that really work. No new subsidies or complicated applications, but simple and effective policies so children can just participate.

137177.explanation: We are not against subsidies for music associations, but we oppose a scheme where every child automatically receives paid individual music lessons. Support for low-income families should go through existing, efficient programs. New broad schemes cause extra costs and bureaucracy.

137178.explanation: We oppose completely stopping support for top sports events. These can bring Apeldoorn significant economic benefits and profile enhancement. However, support should remain proportional and must not come at the expense of grassroots sports. Balance between elite and grassroots sports is needed.

137179.explanation: We do not believe more enforcement officers (BOAs) are automatically needed. First, bureaucracy must be reduced and practical approaches restored. Residents benefit more from a visible neighborhood police officer who knows the area than from more enforcement officers. Therefore, we choose to strengthen neighborhood police, not increase BOAs.

137180.explanation: We believe Apeldoorn has enough coffee shops. First, the current supply must remain safe and functional without extra pressure on neighborhoods or enforcement. Adding new coffee shops has no clear benefit.

137181.explanation: We oppose giving pepper spray to BOAs because this is not legally permitted. The focus should be on proper equipment within their powers and de-escalation. This way, BOAs can perform their work safely and appropriately without unnecessary escalation.

137182.explanation: We support more lighting on squares and places with social functions. In parks, we prefer tailored solutions: lighting where safety requires it, but consciously leaving space for residents who want darkness and peace, provided good alternative routes are available.

137183.explanation: We believe the municipality should urge the province for proper wildlife management. In some cases, this may mean that a wolf is culled. It concerns safety, balance in nature, and clear action. In emergencies, the mayor should have more powers to protect people and animals.

137184.explanation: Traditions such as carbide shooting and fireworks should be allowed to continue during New Year's Eve. However, with common sense and consideration for the environment. In some places, a fireworks- or carbide-free zone may be appropriate, but no general ban. Tailored solutions instead of nannying.

137185.explanation: We oppose a total ban on fatbikes in the city center. Extra bans solve little. Behavior and rule compliance are what matter. Restrictions in genuine walking areas are logical. Otherwise, we prefer better supervision and enforcement instead of nannying and new rules.

137186.explanation: We support cameras at waste collection points to prevent illegal dumping. Deployment must be targeted and functional: only at the collection point itself. Privacy must be well protected, and no more data collected than necessary. This keeps the environment clean without unnecessary intrusion.

137187.explanation: We oppose a fixed 40% quota for social housing. Housing construction must fit the needs of a neighborhood or village. Rigid quotas can delay construction. We primarily want affordable housing and a logical mix per area so that houses are really built where there is demand.

137188.explanation: We support converting vacant offices into housing if it contributes to increasing affordable living space. Municipal support should be targeted and responsible, enhancing feasibility. Only projects that fit the environment and remain affordable deserve support.

137189.explanation: We support allowing permanent residence in holiday homes, as long as it suits the situation. You are currently facing a housing shortage, and smart solutions must be utilized. However, this must not come at the expense of tourism or the livability of the area and surrounding villages.

137192.explanation: We oppose restricting taller buildings only to the railway area. We choose tailored solutions: in some places more height can fit, as long as it suits the neighborhood and matches the surroundings. This maintains balance. A growing city requires more housing per square meter.

137193.explanation: We support demolishing the former Centraal Beheer building. The building is iconic but contributes too little in its current state. This site is urgently needed for new housing. Not preserved for the sake of preservation, but choosing what advances Apeldoorn.

137194.explanation: We oppose a complete ban on housing construction on agricultural land. Infill alone is not always enough but is step one. If agricultural land is used, it should be without coercion, appropriate to the area, and without villages growing into the city. Careful and sparing use of space is paramount.

137195.explanation: We oppose reducing support for people with low incomes. Everyone must be able to participate in Apeldoorn. However, funds must be used sustainably and purposefully. Help remains necessary, but then effective, useful, and future-proof.

137196.explanation: We believe socially meaningful work can add value for people receiving welfare benefits. It must never be a punishment. It's about motivating and guiding participation. Those genuinely unable due to health should not feel additional pressure. Support where possible, rest where necessary.

137198.explanation: We disagree that people undergoing retraining should not have to apply for jobs at all. Job applications must be meaningful and only when there is a real chance of suitable work. Retraining often creates new opportunities that can be utilized.

137200.explanation: We oppose free public transport for people with low incomes. That is unaffordable and unhelpful in villages without good public transport connections. We choose tailored solutions that fit individual situations and policies that truly reduce poverty rather than merely addressing symptoms.

137201.explanation: We oppose the indiscriminate removal of parking spaces for extra greenery or playgrounds. A neighborhood must have room for all three: greenery, play, and parking. These are basic needs for you and your community. Designing a neighborhood should balance these properly.

137202.explanation: Parking in the city center must remain affordable. Many residents, especially from villages and neighborhoods, rely on cars. Excessive fees deter visitors and harm businesses. Affordable parking keeps the center accessible and supports a strong local economy.

137203.explanation: We want the city center to remain easily accessible by car. Many residents, especially from villages and outskirts, depend on their cars. Good accessibility is essential for shops and hospitality. BBB chooses practical solutions, not car-restricted measures that hurt residents and businesses.

137204.explanation: We oppose converting existing parking spaces in residential streets to charging stations. Charging facilities are important but must not come at the cost of residents' daily parking convenience.

137205.explanation: It increases burdens on residents and harms local businesses. Cars should not be seen as a cash cow. Nuisance must be addressed with smart solutions such as agreements with companies, sufficient parking spaces, and enforcement without imposing extra costs on residents.

137206.explanation: We oppose expanding car-free streets in the city center. The center must remain easily accessible for you, businesses, and visitors. Many residents, especially from villages and outskirts, depend on cars. More car-free streets make the city less accessible.

137207.explanation: We believe traffic measures must make sense. 30 km/h is appropriate in residential streets, near schools and care facilities where safety is paramount, but not on all roads. Main roads must maintain their traffic flow function. A general 30 km/h rule causes detours. BBB chooses tailored solutions.

137208.explanation: We want Apeldoorn to remain well accessible and new infrastructure to be logically designed. A rail tunnel requires a suitable feeder road. By giving Laan van Spitsbergen 2×2 lanes near the tunnel, traffic flows smoothly and emergency response times improve.

137209.explanation: Separated bike paths increase traffic safety, especially along busy roads and in rural areas where cars drive faster. By separating cyclists and motorized traffic, we improve safety and flow for everyone in Apeldoorn.

137210.explanation: Cyclists and pedestrians should get green lights more often where logical, such as towards the center and near schools, care facilities, and shopping centers. Safety must come first there. This keeps traffic logical and safe.

137211.explanation: We oppose a ban on wood burning. Those who use clean and dry wood should be able to continue. A wood stove is also important as a backup during power outages. We rely on common sense and consideration for the surroundings, not on nannying and bans.

137212.explanation: BBB supports increasing the informal care allowance from €50 to €150 per year. Informal care is often more than a job and deserves appreciation and support. Those who are there daily for a loved one should not be left alone and must be able to get help when it becomes too much.

137213.explanation: We disagree that people receiving welfare benefits who are informal caregivers must always keep applying for jobs. Job applications must be meaningful and only when there is a real chance of suitable work. Informal care requires time and effort, and there must be room for that.

137214.explanation: We support more shelter places for homeless people, but shelter must not be an end goal. It is a safety net, not a final destination. The municipality must also focus on prevention, such as debt management, guidance, and affordable housing to reduce homelessness.

137215.explanation: We oppose stopping hunting on municipal lands. Good wildlife management is necessary to keep nature in balance and protect vulnerable animals. It is not about recreational hunting, but responsible intervention when populations get out of hand and damage or suffering threatens.

137216.explanation: New construction outside built-up areas must be possible. Infill alone is not always sufficient. Expansion near villages can be allowed if it is small-scale, fits the landscape, and occurs without forced buyouts. This way we tackle the housing shortage without villages growing into the city.

137217.explanation: We support helping entrepreneurs make their buildings more accessible for people with disabilities. This must remain realistic and affordable, taking into account the entrepreneur’s role. Support is logical where it has an effect and truly helps people participate.

233683.explanation: We do not want to increase property taxes but rather decrease them. First, the municipality must get its budget in order and stop prestige projects. Tax relief must be genuine and not be recouped from residents through other levies.

233684.explanation: The municipality should not spend its own money on tasks imposed by the national government. When The Hague creates something, sufficient funds must accompany it. Residents must not pay for deficits. Help remains available, but costs advanced by us must be reimbursed by the national government.

233686.explanation: We oppose co-financing heat networks. They are expensive, offer little freedom of choice, and bring financial risks for residents. Municipal funds must go to solutions that provide immediate help, not uncertain large-scale projects. BBB chooses affordability and freedom of choice.

233687.explanation: Terraces should be allowed to stay open longer. Entrepreneurs deserve space to do business, and we trust in personal responsibility. However, it must fit the area and be well-coordinated to prevent nuisance. This keeps it enjoyable and livable for everyone.

233688.explanation: In disasters, the municipality must be there for you. Extra support points and shelters are not a luxury but a necessity. Safety and preparedness are core municipal tasks. You must be able to get fast and reliable assistance in emergency situations.

233689.explanation: Apeldoorn should forego large-scale housing development on the other side of the A1 and A50 highways. These open areas are valuable for agriculture, nature, and livability and are furthermore low and wet. First, utilize what already exists: vacancies, conversions, and smart densification near existing villages.

233690.explanation: The municipality must facilitate housing forms for the elderly. You have the right to suitable homes near care, shops, and facilities. Initiatives like senior cohousing help you live independently longer and promote housing turnover. Aging should be possible in your own neighborhood or village.

233691.explanation: Apeldoorn should stop formally being a rainbow municipality. You should be able to be yourself without labels. Safety, respect, and equal treatment are part of normal municipal policy for everyone, including LGBTIQA+ residents. This should be arranged broadly and structurally, not through a separate label.

233694.explanation: People with confused behavior must be helped quickly and appropriately. If you or someone around you is struggling, help must be nearby. More funding may be needed, but only if used pragmatically. No extra bureaucracy, but clear, people-centered help that truly works.

137153.explanation: The municipal referendum is an important tool for residents to express their opinions on key municipal decisions. The threshold for this must be lowered, especially in times of eroding support for the municipality.

137156.explanation: Where associations can manage things, the municipality can be relieved. Good realistic agreements with sports clubs are essential for this.

137158.explanation: Dog tax is a purpose-specific tax, and abolishing it entirely means all residents (including non-dog owners) will pay for costs related to dog walking areas, etc. That is too far for us. Moreover, Apeldoorn has already reduced dog tax in recent years.

137159.explanation: Businesses belong in Apeldoorn. BVA supports the Apeldoorn South Business Association in developing a future-oriented business park with space for innovation and cooperation.

137160.explanation: BVA believes in the autonomy of our residents and good dialogue between the municipality and hospitality industry. Differentiated closing times help prevent all visitors from being outside at the same time, with all the consequences that entails.

137161.explanation: Too often being open on Sundays puts pressure on small shopkeepers. The municipality should engage in dialogue with the retail sector and not allow random openings.

137162.explanation: BVA wants agricultural land to remain untouched. Nature areas are, like agricultural land, cultural landscapes. Therefore, no large-scale housing or solar fields on agricultural land.

137164.explanation: BVA chooses to support residents for social initiatives, not for unprofitable businesses.

137165.explanation: Residents of Apeldoorn with ties to the city deserve priority over newcomers. Once waiting lists for social housing and primary care are cleared, efforts can be made towards distribution.

137166.explanation: BVA opts for a forestry policy based on common sense, not on ideological automatisms.

137167.explanation: BVA supports the initiative fund to help residents who want to improve the livability of their own environment.

137168.explanation: Solar panels impact our environment. For energy generation, it may be necessary to place them on buildings. BVA rejects damage to our landscape, including agricultural land.

137169.explanation: BVA is clearly against the arbitrariness of an environmental zone that excludes small businesses and Apeldoorn residents who cannot afford electric vehicles.

137171.explanation: Tourists are important to us and they know how to find Apeldoorn well. Reducing (tourist) taxes and improving signage help more than spending money on campaigns from expensive agencies.

137172.explanation: BVA stands fully for privacy. Collecting data about people without their consent is not part of that. Also due to additional radiation and other spatial effects. No 'white boxes' 2.0 in Apeldoorn.

137173.explanation: The Zwitsalterrein has long been known as the event site near the city center. As long as the nuisance remains limited, this should continue.

137174.explanation: Integration concerns people with a status. For BVA, autonomy and self-reliance are the starting points. Apeldoorn’s capacity is not unlimited. Spending even more money on integration goes at the expense of livability, safety, and social cohesion in neighborhoods and villages.

137176.explanation: Sports are healthy. BVA wants to encourage that by supporting sports clubs, not by steering based on income, which excludes middle groups.

137177.explanation: BVA finds musical education important. That requires trust in and support for music associations, not income-based aid that excludes middle groups.

137178.explanation: BVA prioritizes grassroots sports. Elite sports can serve as a role model. Completely stopping funding for elite sports goes too far.

137179.explanation: BVA wants more police on the streets. A BOA (special enforcement officer) is not a police officer. Quickly fining those causing nuisance can help.

137180.explanation: Apeldoorn already has enough coffee shops.

137181.explanation: BVA chooses BOA officers who can safely perform their duties with adequate police support. Assigning tasks to BOAs for which they are not trained and equipping them with weapons can lead to unwanted escalation.

137182.explanation: Lighting can increase safety in public spaces. Considering safety in design is more important.

137183.explanation: BVA supports protecting residents. Since high fences do not prevent danger from wolves, active population management fits.

137184.explanation: Cabidschieten (a traditional shooting activity) is part of the tradition in our rural area. It takes place under supervision. There is no reason for an outright ban.

137185.explanation: Fatbikes do not belong in the city center among shopping crowds. Without a helmet requirement, it is unsafe and puts an undue burden on our enforcement officers and residents.

137186.explanation: Cameras are no panacea. The current waste policy makes it difficult for residents to dispose of waste properly and leads to nuisance and street pollution. This does not improve with cameras that invade privacy.

137187.explanation: BVA supports housing corporations doing what they are intended for: providing social housing. Setting unrealistic requirements does not help and deters builders. We believe in good cooperation and agreements with housing corporations.

137188.explanation: Converting offices into homes is costly and should be left to the market. The municipality can actively explore these possibilities. Demolition and new construction often prove the cheapest and most efficient solution.

137189.explanation: Living in holiday parks can be a temporary solution due to the housing shortage. The pilot for permanent residence in holiday homes has ended. The municipality should actively cooperate on alternatives and housing for caravan residents.

137192.explanation: BVA supports preserving the character of Apeldoorn. High-rise buildings only suit the station area near Saxion University and Aventus MBO. Not in neighborhoods. With the demolition of the dilapidated Herzberger building, housing can be realized quickly and relatively cheaply.

137193.explanation: BVA is in favor of iconic new housing by demolishing this rundown building. Spending more money on impossible plans at this station location is irresponsible according to BVA.

137194.explanation: BVA opposes large-scale housing on agricultural land. Building bakehouses, care homes, and development on one's own land clearly do not fall under this.

137195.explanation: BVA considers it fundamentally wrong to shift (energy) costs from the national government onto the municipality. Compassion remains an important principle, so this cannot be reduced significantly.

137196.explanation: Residents without work receive benefits. As long as they are not disabled, they can do socially meaningful work or be helped with training or gaining experience to get back to work.

137198.explanation: Education can help find work but does not replace it.

137200.explanation: Free travel is pleasant for everyone. BVA opposes municipal income politics. Public transport cost increases are caused by inflation and taxes imposed by the national government. It is fundamentally wrong to lay the consequences with municipalities.

137201.explanation: Parking on private property is not possible for everyone. Too few parking spaces on the street lead to unsafe traffic situations, also because cars block access for emergency services. Parking must not be neglected in residential area design.

137202.explanation: BVA chooses a better accessible city center. This includes cheaper parking (blue card for short-term parking).

137203.explanation: A well-accessible city center is essential for Apeldoorn’s residents and entrepreneurs. BVA wants this established in a new plan (Traffic in Apeldoorn).

137204.explanation: If people switch to electric vehicles on a large scale, gas stations will adapt. Designating charging spots in streets and neighborhoods can cause nuisance and lead to procedures.

137205.explanation: BVA supports parking freedom. When developing neighborhoods and designing streets, the municipality should consider a sufficiently high parking standard (= number of parking spaces per dwelling).

137206.explanation: Accessibility of the city center is essential for entrepreneurs and residents. Harassing cars does not help. Traffic policy should be based on measuring, evaluating, and adjusting, not ideology.

137207.explanation: Restrictions on private transport, such as zero-emission zones or 30 km/h speed limits on the city center ring, undermine free movement. Traffic policy should be based on measuring, evaluating, and adjusting, not ideology.

137208.explanation: Smooth traffic flow serves safety. BVA supports a new traffic route plan for Apeldoorn to avoid traffic nuisance and enhance safety. With common sense. Small-scale local transport fits this approach.

137209.explanation: The safety of cyclists, especially students and commuters, benefits from clear traffic rules and well-lit bike paths. Given the changing nature of cycling traffic, this is BVA's priority. No more major overhauls.

137210.explanation: Traffic lights should be set to ensure safe and smooth traffic flow. This requires expertise, not ideology.

137211.explanation: BVA is critical of all-encompassing bans. In times of rising (climate) costs and the transition away from natural gas, it is anti-social to forbid people from heating in alternative ways. Good air quality remains important.

137212.explanation: Caregivers perform an important societal role. After years of cuts to allowances, increasing the compensation is humane and desirable. The amount involved is relatively small.

137213.explanation: BVA believes caregivers fulfill an important social function. Too strict job application requirements lead to fraud and do not encourage employment. BVA supports humane individualized solutions.

137214.explanation: BVA opts for active policies against the growth of homelessness, by reducing burdens and discouraging labor migration.

137215.explanation: Active wildlife management may make hunting necessary.

137216.explanation: BVA does not support large-scale new construction in rural areas for newcomers. However, small-scale new building on own property is supported.

137217.explanation: Meeting accessibility requirements should not depend on government funding. However, the requirements should be tightened.

233683.explanation: The municipality has a budget of over 800 million. BVA strives for a smaller, more efficient municipality, thus with lower taxes.

233684.explanation: BVA thinks the municipality itself should determine how funds are spent, regardless of what the national government does.

233686.explanation: There is insufficient support among Apeldoorn residents for heat networks. It costs the municipality at least 30 million euros plus an equal amount in guarantees. In the long run, billions. BVA finds it completely irresponsible to continue with this.

233687.explanation: Opening hours can be better aligned through consultation with hospitality entrepreneurs and consumer needs.

233688.explanation: BVA currently sees no reason to do more than the municipality already does. If it turns out (e.g. after a referendum) that residents desire it, we will support it.

233689.explanation: BVA wants to build at the station location and favors infill and small-scale housing projects (for seniors). BVA opposes the growth ambition to attract people from outside while the basics for Apeldoorn residents are not yet in order.

233690.explanation: BVA notes that the lack of housing for seniors is a major cause of the housing market stagnation in Apeldoorn. When seniors can move within their familiar neighborhood, families and young people can also find a place.

233691.explanation: A rainbow municipality is not an end in itself. The municipality belongs to everyone and should refrain from political expressions. Discrimination must be countered.

233694.explanation: Due to national policies, problems with people with confused behavior increasingly fall to the municipality. Simply solving this with municipal money is, in BVA’s view, too simplistic.

statements.theme: Paid parking in the city centre
statements.title: Paid parking in the center should be made cheaper.
statements.pro: Some parties think cheaper parking makes it more attractive to come to the city center by car. It is also said that many residents depend on cars. Furthermore, parties believe cheaper parking is good for downtown businesses.
statements.con: Other parties argue that cheaper parking leads visitors to come less often on foot, by bike, or by public transport. There is also a party that believes parking rates in Apeldoorn are not high. Finally, one party argues that cheaper parking will not, or will hardly, lead to more visitors to the city center.

statements.title: The former Centraal Beheer building on Prins Willem-Alexanderlaan should be demolished.
statements.moreInfo: the former Centraal Beheer building is part of an office complex from 1972 designed by Herman Hertzberger and is now owned by the municipality. The municipality is exploring new functions for the building. The associated high office towers will be demolished. The Cultural Heritage Agency of the Netherlands wants the building to become a national monument.
statements.pro: Some parties think it is too difficult to convert the former Centraal Beheer building into a residential building that meets current standards. It is also said that preserving the building entails large financial risks. Furthermore, parties believe the building is outdated.
statements.con: Other parties believe that the internationally known building attracts people to come to Apeldoorn. There is also a party that thinks the building is valuable heritage connected to Apeldoorn's history. Finally, parties argue that the housing shortage can be effectively addressed in this building.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.pro: Some parties believe the dog tax discourages dog ownership, whereas dogs actually help combat loneliness. It is also said that it is unfair for dog owners to contribute to facilities used by all residents. Furthermore, one party believes that collecting a dog tax costs more than it yields.
statements.con: Other parties think dog owners should contribute to the costs of dog exercise fields. There are also parties that believe income from dog tax is needed for liveability and the performance of core tasks in Apeldoorn. Finally, one party argues that it is unfair if all residents have to pay for facilities used only by dog owners.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.moreInfo: Homeowners can install solar panels on their roof if it is suitable. If that is not possible, people can join others to buy or rent land for one or more wind turbines or solar parks so they can still invest in sustainable energy. Sustainable energy is generated from sources that never run out, such as sunlight and wind. Collective generation of sustainable energy often takes place in the form of energy cooperatives.
statements.pro: Some parties think municipal support for generating sustainable energy accelerates the transition to renewable energy sources. It is also said that low-income households will have easier access to sustainable energy this way. Furthermore, one party believes support for sustainable energy strengthens engagement and public support for sustainable solutions.
statements.con: Other parties believe there are already sufficient national schemes for generating sustainable energy. There are also parties that think residents should pay the costs themselves of generating sustainable energy. Finally, one party argues that the municipality should not support unprofitable enterprises.

statements.title: The municipality should stop spending money on top-level sporting events.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geen geld meer steken in

titleParts.glossary: Tournaments or series of competitions in which the best athletes and teams in the world compete against each other for prizes.

statements.pro: Some parties believe top-level sporting events cost a lot of money. It is also said that these kinds of events bring nothing to the municipality. Furthermore, one party believes top sports events only reach a limited group of residents.
statements.con: Other parties believe top sports events inspire people to start exercising themselves. There are also parties that think these kinds of events can generate extra income for entrepreneurs in Apeldoorn. Finally, one party argues that top sports events are an enjoyable experience for residents and tourists.

statements.title: The speed limit on all roads within the central ring should be 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.

titleParts.text: moet op alle wegen binnen de centrumring 30 km/u worden.

statements.pro: Some parties believe a 30 km/h speed limit is safer for cyclists and pedestrians. It is also said that this creates more space for greenery, improving air quality. Furthermore, parties believe a lower speed limit makes the city center more liveable.
statements.con: Other parties argue that a lower maximum speed within the city ring causes unnecessary traffic movements because people will take detours. There are also parties that think a 30 km/h limit is only needed on residential streets and near schools and care institutions. Finally, one party argues that this measure does not outweigh the costs.

statements.title: The municipality should allow new coffeeshops in Apeldoorn.

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.pro: Some parties believe there is a need for an additional coffeeshop in Apeldoorn. Furthermore, parties think extra coffeeshops need not be a problem as long as they are not located near schools.
statements.con: Other parties believe that additional coffeeshops add nothing. Finally, parties argue that coffeeshops often cause nuisance in the surrounding area.

statements.title: Apeldoorn should increase the compensation for informal caregivers from €50 to €150 per year.

titleParts.glossary: People who provide unpaid and often long-term care for sick family members or friends.

statements.pro: Some parties think increasing the carer’s allowance is an appropriate token of appreciation for a demanding task. It is also said that this measure helps prevent carers from becoming overburdened. Furthermore, parties believe caregiving is more than a job.
statements.con: Other parties believe practical support is more effective than a symbolic allowance. There is also a party that thinks it is a token of appreciation rather than a payment. Finally, parties argue that caregivers are better helped with respite care than with an allowance.

statements.title: More car-free streets should be created in Apeldoorn's city centre.
statements.pro: Some parties think car-free streets make the city center much more pleasant. It is also said that less car traffic provides space for more greenery, which has a positive effect on people's health. Furthermore, parties believe the city center is for pedestrians and cyclists.
statements.con: Other parties consider it very important for entrepreneurs, residents and visitors that the city center remains easily accessible by car. There is also a party that believes more car-free streets are unnecessary because the shopping area is getting smaller. Finally, one party thinks more car-free streets are only possible if there is sufficient free or very cheap public transport.

statements.title: If the municipality cuts down a tree, two new ones must be planted.
statements.moreInfo: The obligation to plant two new trees would only apply if the municipality fells a tree that stands on municipal land; it would not apply to trees outside municipal land.
statements.pro: Some parties believe trees are important for the climate and liveability. It is also said that the municipality should set a good example for its residents. Furthermore, one party believes this keeps the municipality's green spaces at a good level.
statements.con: Other parties find a fixed rule of two new trees for each felled tree undesirable. There is also a party that believes the municipality should not introduce extra rules. Finally, one party argues that this depends on the location.

statements.title: The municipality should distribute holders of refugee status across all neighborhoods and villages in the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Refugees who have been granted a residence permit and are therefore allowed to stay in the Netherlands.

titleParts.text: spreiden over alle wijken en dorpen in de gemeente.

statements.pro: Some parties believe dispersal promotes the integration of refugees with residence permits. Furthermore, parties believe this preserves liveability in all neighborhoods and villages.
statements.con: Other parties believe that status holders are ordinary residents who do not need special treatment. Finally, one party argues that when spreading them around, there is not always good consideration of what a neighborhood can handle.

statements.title: The Laan van Spitsbergen should be widened to two lanes in each direction at the location of the planned rail tunnel.
statements.moreInfo: Laan van Spitsbergen is part of Apeldoorn’s ring road. The municipality plans to replace the level railway crossing there with a tunnel with 2×1 lanes, but there is debate about whether the tunnel should immediately be widened to 2×2 lanes.
statements.pro: Some parties believe a two-by-two lane layout is better for traffic safety. There are also parties who think traffic will flow better that way. Furthermore, one party believes the municipality should tackle this bottleneck properly in one go.
statements.con: Other parties find two-by-two lanes too expensive. It is also said that this measure shifts the traffic jam to the ring road. Finally, parties argue that widening the tunnel is not necessary at all.

statements.title: The municipality should stop hunting wild animals on municipal land.
statements.moreInfo: In the Netherlands hunting is allowed for some wild animals. Hunting large game such as roe deer, red deer and wild boar is permitted only with provincial approval. As a landowner the municipality of Apeldoorn can choose to prohibit hunting on municipal land.
statements.pro: Some parties believe all wild animals deserve respect. It is also said that all animals contribute to biodiversity. Furthermore, parties believe the municipality must safeguard animals' right to life.
